description: Define an Ubuntu Server 24.04 LTS autoinstall ISO build layout for the Alga appliance.
Create autoinstall configuration for unattended Ubuntu installation with opinionated partitioning, base user, packages, and first-boot service enablement.
Package host-level Alga appliance setup/status service artifacts into the ISO-installed Ubuntu host.
Create systemd unit for the durable host web setup/status service on port 8080.
Create systemd unit or equivalent startup path for console fallback that works from VM console and serial-console-style access without becoming the only setup path.
Generate and persist a setup/status token with restricted permissions on first boot.
Display detected node IP, setup URL, setup token, fallback instructions, and log locations on the VM console after first boot.
Implement token-protected web setup route at /setup on host port 8080.
Implement setup form fields for channel, app URL/hostname, DNS servers, and support/testing repo override values.
Default setup channel to stable and clearly mark nightly as testing/support-directed.
Default DNS mode to DHCP/system-provided resolvers when available, make DNS configuration prominent, and allow deliberate custom DNS overrides such as 8.8.8.8,8.8.4.4.
Implement console fallback prompts that collect the same required setup values as the web setup UI and share the same validation/setup engine.
Persist setup inputs and install state under host appliance configuration/state paths with restricted permissions for sensitive values.
Implement setup engine preflight phases for validating DNS, GitHub channel/release access, GHCR reachability, and proxy/egress behavior before installing k3s or mutating the host.
Install opinionated single-node k3s from the setup engine with pinned version and expected kubeconfig path.
Disable unneeded bundled k3s components such as Traefik and ServiceLB unless required by final implementation validation.
Ensure local-path storage is available and default for appliance persistent volumes.
Install Flux into the k3s cluster from the setup engine.
Resolve stable/nightly channel metadata directly from the GitHub repo for v1 online installs.
Create Flux GitRepository and Kustomization resources pointing at the Alga GitHub repo and selected channel branch/path.
Normalize GitHub SSH-style repository defaults to public HTTPS URLs for Flux source creation.
Apply runtime values and appliance release-selection configuration for the selected channel/release.
Transition host port 8080 from setup mode into durable status mode after setup begins/completes.
Port or reimplement status collection from the Talos in-cluster status plane to the Ubuntu host service using the local k3s kubeconfig.
Report readiness tiers: platform, core, bootstrap, login, background, and fully healthy.
Keep email, Temporal, workflow-worker, and temporal-worker from blocking login readiness.
Classify setup/status failures by network, DNS, GitHub/release source, k3s, Flux, storage, app bootstrap, app readiness, and background services.
Show current phase, last action, logs, suspected cause, suggested next step, and retry safety in the status UI.

Implement first-class support bundle generation from the host status service, including host service logs, setup/update logs, k3s state, Kubernetes resources/events, Flux/Helm state, app bootstrap logs, network diagnostics, disk usage, release metadata, and secret redaction.
Implement app-only channel update flow from the host status UI for stable and nightly.
Ensure app update flow resolves channel metadata, applies values/release selection, and requests Flux/Helm reconciliation without automating OS or k3s updates.
Document that Ubuntu package and k3s updates are manual/support-run in v1, name the CVE/operations liability explicitly, and sketch the v2 managed maintenance direction.
Document new-user install flow for VMware ESXi/cloud VM users with Ubuntu ISO, setup URL, DNS defaults, and bootstrap expectations.
prdRefs: New install: VMware ESXi or cloud VM

description: Document upgrade flow for app-only channel updates through the host status UI.
Persist host, Ubuntu, k3s, and app version/maintenance metadata needed for a future managed OS/k3s update flow.
Make release-source preflight failures visible in setup UI before install begins, with explicit proxy/firewall/DNS guidance.

Remove or retire Talos-specific appliance implementation paths from the supported v1 appliance product surface, including Talos bootstrap/operator flows, Talos machine config assumptions, Talos-specific docs, and Talos-specific status dependencies, while preserving reusable release/channel/status logic in the Ubuntu implementation.

Add clear internal and external documentation that Ubuntu replaces Talos as the supported appliance OS path and that existing Talos appliance artifacts are legacy/unsupported unless explicitly handled by support.
